headline: Pahang Logging Road Network Expanded 340km into Virgin Forest
context: Satellite analysis by the Rimba Collective revealed 340km of new logging roads carved into previously unroaded primary forest in Pahang's Taman Negara buffer zone during 2024.

cards.big: Taman Negara is 130 million years old — the world's oldest rainforest.
cards.sub: 340km of new logging roads now penetrate its buffer zone, opening previously inaccessible areas to extraction.

cards.big: Buffer zones have no legal protection under the National Forestry Act 1984.
cards.sub: Only gazette national parks are protected. Buffer zones rely on state discretion alone.

cards.big: Roads increase poaching access. Tiger sightings near new roads dropped 67%.
cards.sub: Peninsular Malaysia has fewer than 150 wild tigers. Each road is a corridor for poachers.

cards.big: Pahang state earned RM142M in logging royalties from buffer zone concessions.
cards.sub: Federal compensation to states for foregoing logging — promised under the Central Forest Spine plan — was never disbursed.

cards.big: A buffer zone that can be logged is not a buffer. It is a queue.
Protecting Taman Negara requires legally protecting its buffer. The federal government must either gazette the buffer or pay states not to log it. Moral appeals alone have demonstrably failed.
